AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. GREGORY WOOD, Esq. of Wood Litigation, APC (tel: 415-247-7900) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. NINA DINDRAL-PAMIDI, Esq. of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP (tel: 714-426-4600) for Defendant PJD Construction, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PJD CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE.

Matter not argued / contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications:

Defendant PJD seeks to strike the complaint's prayer for punitive damages against PJD. This motion is denied without prejudice as unripe. The court has sustained PJD's demurrer as to four of the causes of action alleged against it but has given Plaintiffs leave to amend. PJD may bring the motion as to any amended complaint or as to the complaint in its present form again if Plaintiffs elect not to amend. The parties must meet and confer in advance of any renewed motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
